summary.what: Unify the cross-agent-debt gate across all hooks and resolve --anchor to a SHA at write time
summary.why: A bug report on parallel-agent worktree repos showed the claude-stop hook blocking session end while 'timbers pending' reported clean. Root cause wasn't the report's hypothesis (transcript scanning / linear-vs-docSet): the Stop hook was the one gate that ignored TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT, and its 'manual run exits 0' was just the empty-stdin parse bailout masking that it never ran the check. Two adjacent real defects rode along: --anchor HEAD was stored as the literal 'HEAD' (defeating the since-anchor model), and 'timbers log' tripped its own pre-commit gate on sibling debt, failing with 'failed to commit entry file' — the act of paying the debt blocked by the debt gate.
summary.how: Rejected the report's ask to make the hook and 'pending' share one computation — the gate (first-parent) and display (full-DAG) are deliberately different so agent A isn't blocked by agent B's merged debt; collapsing them would regress that. Instead: (1) claude-stop honors the same env-var opt-out the sibling hooks do, with the const now aliased from ledger.SkipCrossAgentDebtEnv for one source of truth; (2) 'timbers log' resolves symbolic --anchor via a new mockable GitOps.ResolveCommit before storage, erroring on unresolvable refs instead of writing phantom entries; (3) the entry commit sets TIMBERS_SKIP_CROSS_AGENT_DEBT=1 on its git invocation (not --no-verify, so foreign hooks still run) to self-exempt. Test-first for all three failure modes; independent review found nothing.

notes: Considered splitting into three commits (one per bead) but git.go carries helpers for two of them and non-interactive staging can't cleanly split a file; landed as one cohesive, revertable-as-a-unit commit referencing all three beads. Also considered adding a .timbers-absent guard to claude-stop to mirror the other hooks — dropped as redundant since the latest==nil path in HasPendingCommits already yields no-block.
